channel.title: Home on Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club

item.title: Activities & Media
item.description:
Welcome to the BSARC Activities and Media gallery! Click any event title below to expand its photo grid. Click any thumbnail image inside to view the full-resolution photo in a new tab.

📅 2025 Events

🎄 Annual Christmas Party

📅 2024 Events

⛺ ARRL Field Day (Calabash Park)
⛺ ARRL Winter Field Day (Saint James Park)

📅 2023 Events

🏕️ Light House On The Air

📅 2008 Events

🏕️ Oak Island Lighthouse 50th Anniversary

item.title: Club Asset Inventory

Radio Yaesu FT-710 N/A 📍 closet 1 💰 For Sale ($650) Purchase 💰

item.title: Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES)
item.description:
Brunswick County ARRL ARES Program

The Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES) consists of licensed radio amateurs who have voluntarily registered their qualifications and equipment for auxiliary communications duties under the national ARRL leadership matrix.

Every licensed amateur, regardless of membership in the ARRL or any other local organization, is eligible to join. Our primary mission is securing emergency communications support loops for non-governmental welfare and humanitarian field units (such as the American Red Cross and local shelters) during community emergencies.

item.title: Auxiliary Communications (AUXCOMM)
item.description:
Brunswick County Government AUXCOMM Infrastructure

The Auxiliary Communications (AUXCOMM) unit is a formally recognized public safety component integrated directly into the Brunswick County Emergency Management Agency under the regulatory guidance of CISA and the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).

Unlike private organizational programs, AUXCOMM hams are formally registered auxiliary government volunteers mobilized under state directives to execute direct communications support inside local municipal assets—including Emergency Operations Centers (EOCs), mobile public safety command trailers, and municipal 911 dispatch centers during official county disaster declarations.

item.title: BSARC Weekly Bulletin Archive
item.description:
Welcome to the Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club official bulletin repository. Weekly updates are compiled by the club secretary to summarize localized emergency traffic nets, public safety coordination, upcoming VE testing configurations, and general business announcements.

The absolute latest release is pinned live on the main dashboard sidebar desk layout for immediate tracking.

Review our chronological historical log archive below to catch up on any previous announcements, nets, or meeting minutes.

item.title: Active Incident Operations Briefing
item.description:
This dashboard lists real-time tactical status logs, ongoing frequency changes, and emergency traffic update chronologies managed by the Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club emergency management team.

🎯 Current Operation Objective

A tropical storm warning has been issued for Brunswick County. The club net is on standby status on the 147.315 repeater. Listen out for active emergency traffic updates.

item.title: Official Club Calendar
item.description:
This schedule lists all official Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club operations, scheduled VE testing sessions, regional hamfests, public service deployments, and monthly breakfast gatherings.

item.title: Skywarn Severe Weather Operations
item.description:
The Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club works in close synchronization with the National Weather Service (NWS) to provide critical ground-truth verification when severe or tropical weather systems threaten our region.

📡 Operational Procedures & Infrastructure

When hazardous conditions arise, the Brunswick County Skywarn Emergency Coordinators (ECs) will activate our localized spotter network. Spotters on the ground gather vital meteorological information and transmit reports directly to the ECs.

item.title: Equipment Marketplace
item.description:
The club provides this marketplace layout as a service to regional amateur radio operators. Please review the specific contact directives within individual listings to arrange transaction logistics.

📋 Looking for Club Loaner Gear?

Authorized members can explore our full master physical holdings repository vault online.

item.title: N4GM QSL & Callsign History
item.description:
Callsign History

The Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club obtained the N4GM club call on October 13, 2007. The club does not act as QSL manager for any calls other than the club call. Prior to the club receiving the N4GM, apparently the previous owner did have some other calls that were being serviced. The club has no information on these. QSL cards for QSOs with N4GM prior to October 13, 2007, will be returned with regrets.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: New HF SSB Net, ARES Rotations & Welcome Julia KR4LQQ
item.description:
📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our persistent radio networks remain fully configured for your local check-ins:

  • 🎙️ The Shrimp Net: Every Monday morning at 8:30 AM via the 147.315 MHz machine (+600 offset, Tone 118.8 Hz).
  • 🛡️ Brunswick County ARES Net: Every Monday evening at 7:00 PM using the same 147.315 MHz repeater (+600 offset, Tone 118.8 Hz).
  • 🏠 AUXCOM / ARES Operations Meeting: Bi-monthly at 5:00 PM at the BEMC Headquarters Building in Supply, NC. This meeting proceeds directly ahead of the business meeting on months when our main club gather shifts to the Supply facility.

📝 ARES Net Control Station (NCS) Schedule

Activation Date Station Call Sign Last Name First Name
May 25, 2026 KY4HL Donua Erin
June 1, 2026 KR4DHF Wills Matt
June 8, 2026 KI4KK Fryer George
June 15, 2026 N4PEN Sirginson Bill

📻 New Experimental Monthly HF SSB Net

You are cordially invited to join an experimental high-frequency operational pipeline coordinated by Gary Quinn (NC4S) designed to gauge community traffic interest and sharpen local propagation tracking.

item.title: ARRL Membership Support
item.description:
ARRL Membership Application & Renewal Program

Did you know you can support BSARC directly when joining or renewing your ARRL membership? As an official ARRL Affiliated Club, we are commissioned to process applications locally.

When you route your enrollment through the club, the ARRL passes a portion of your standard dues back to our treasury to help fund local repeater maintenance and public service equipment—at absolutely zero extra cost to you!

item.title: Club Roster
item.description:
BSARC Active Membership Directory

Welcome to the official membership roll for the Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club.

To maintain strict operational privacy and shield our members from automated marketing networks, personal telephone lines, mailing addresses, and direct email targets are completely hidden from web crawlers.

Licensed amateur operators can coordinate directly with any club member listed below by clicking their callsign link to access their verified profile environment on QRZ.

item.title: FCC License Renewal Guide
item.description:
Amateur Radio License Renewal Information

Maintaining your FCC license is essential to staying legally active on the airwaves. While you may receive third-party mailings or emails offering to renew your license for an extra service fee, you can easily handle the process yourself.

⚠️ Important FCC Fee Notice: While the process used to be free, the FCC implemented a mandatory $35.00 application fee for amateur license renewals, new licenses, and vanity callsign requests. This fee is paid directly to the FCC via their online portal.

item.title: New Member Guide
item.description:
Welcome to the Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club!

Welcome to BSARC! This guide is designed to help new and prospective members get plugged into our club infrastructure, learn about our local emergency services, and connect with our committee leaders.

Club Officers & Leadership (2026)

To protect our leadership team from automated phishing and spam networks, personal telephone numbers and direct emails are kept secure. Licensed hams can contact any officer instantly by clicking their callsign to view their verified profile on QRZ.

item.title: Officers & Committees
item.description:
🎖️ Club Leadership & Volunteers

The leadership team and committee chairs work hard behind the scenes to keep the club running smoothly, organize public service operations, coordinate community testing, and handle event logistics.

🏛️ Club Officers (2026)

Position / Role Name QRZ Profile

📋 Committees & Appointed Positions

Video & Photography Mary Freeman KQ4NJZ

item.title: Repeaters
item.description:
BSARC Repeater Infrastructure & Net Operations

The Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club maintains and operates localized coordination repeaters to provide open, reliable emergency and hobby communications arrays across southeastern North Carolina.

Our systems are open for deployment use by all licensed amateur radio operators in the region.

item.title: Useful Radio Links
item.description:
Amateur Radio Web Directory

A comprehensive collection of essential amateur radio tools, federal databases, equipment modifications, and surrounding regional club portals. Local interest portals and emergency services links are highlighted below.

Technical Tools & Calculators

  • 🧮 Amateur Radio RF Exposure Safety Calculator — Quick power density analysis to verify station compliance.
  • 🔧 Modifications for Ham Rigs (Mods.dk) — Manuals, schematics, and alignment steps for commercial transceivers.
  • 📻 Nostalgia Air Antique Radio Resource — Vintage tube data files and schematic archives for historic gear restorers.
  • ⏰ NIST WWV Time Signal Station Portal — Official station operational guidelines and transmission details.

National & State Training Organizations

  • 🏛️ ARRL Main Portal — National association for amateur radio in the US.
  • 🗺️ ARRL North Carolina Section — Local section news, event tracking, and field appointments.
  • 🚨 North Carolina ARES Hub — State emergency network coordination, plans, and training matrices.
  • 🌪️ VoIP SKYWARN / Hurricane Net — Real-time severe weather tactical reporting infrastructure.
  • 💻 FEMA Independent Study Directory — Free online NIMS/ICS core training coursework.

Operating Platforms & Networks

  • 🗺️ EchoLink Portal — Global internet-linking software engine for licensed operators.
  • 🌐 Internet Radio Linking Project (IRLP) — Voice-over-IP radio linking standard utilizing dedicated RF nodes.
  • 💬 eHam.net Portal — Product reviews, active ham forums, community articles, and classified listings.
  • 📦 QSL.net Server Space — Free web hosting and email forwarding hubs dedicated strictly to licensed amateurs.
  • 🛒 QTH.com Swapmeet — Classified ad trading spaces and premium ham radio website hosting pipelines.

Federal & Regulatory Portals

  • 📡 FCC Amateur Radio Service Portal — Core licensing rules and service parameters.
  • 📜 FCC Part 97 Rules Reference Code — Federal regulatory guidelines governing operations.
  • 🗃️ FCC Universal Licensing System (ULS) — Official federal master license database and filing dashboard.

Surrounding Regional Clubs & Systems

  • 🗼 Azalea Coast Amateur Radio Club (AC4RC) — Operating out of the greater Wilmington, NC area.
  • 🔗 Carolina 440 UHF Link System — Interconnected wide-coverage repeater matrix serving NC and SC.
  • 🛰️ Carolina DX Association (CDXA) — Dedicated long-distance communication and contesting group.
  • 🌊 Wilmington VHF Association (N4ILM) — Managing wide-area communication links across southeast NC.
  • ⛰️ Shelby Amateur Radio Club — Organizers of the historic regional autumn hamfest.
  • 🏛️ Asheville Radio Museum — Historic preservation site showing the history of early voice gear.
  • 🎟️ Dayton Hamvention Main Site — The premier national amateur radio convention and swap exhibition.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Hurricane Expo Thanks, May Meeting at BEMC & Dayton Hamvention
item.description:
🌪️ Ready Brunswick Preparedness Expo Success

A huge shout-out and thank you to everyone who stepped up to support the club event at the Ready Brunswick Preparedness Expo on Tuesday, May 5, 2026. Your outstanding dedication and public interaction help ensure our communities remain informed and our operators stand ready if and when disaster strikes. Well done, team!

🏛️ BSARC Monthly Club Meeting Tonight

Don’t forget that this month’s official business meeting takes place tonight!

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Treasurer Volunteer Call, Ready Brunswick Expo & May Meeting Info
item.description:
💼 Urgent Notice: Club Treasurer Position Volunteer Needed

A volunteer is needed to step into the vital role of Club Treasurer, as Ephraim Botnick (WQ4N) will be stepping away at the completion of his current elected term.

This is an exceptional and rewarding opportunity to serve your fellow amateurs. A comprehensive training and hands-on operational turnover will be provided to ensure an absolute smooth transition for the newcomer.

⚠️ Organizational Operations Notice: Per our foundational corporate documents, the Treasurer is one of the three (3) mandatory core executive positions strictly required to sustain continual legal operations. If you have a desire to help look after the club’s financial logging, please contact President Mark Sergent (KA4EVR) or any board member.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Car Show Thanks, Treasurer Search & WCARC Hamfest Info
item.description:
🏎️ Cape Fear Cruisers Car Club Show Success

A huge thank you to all the dedicated operators who supported communication checkpoints at the Cape Fear Cruisers Car Show on Saturday, April 25, 2026. Your voluntary presence and reliable tracking layout made a true public service impact within our community. Outstanding job by all who assisted!

💼 Operational Notice: Club Treasurer Position Vacancy

A volunteer is needed to step into the role of Club Treasurer for the upcoming cycle, as Ephraim Botnick (WQ4N) is preparing to step away at the conclusion of his current term.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Welcome Julia KR4LQQ, ARES Schedule & Treasurer Position Notice
item.description:
👋 Welcome Our Newest Member!

Please extend a warm Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club welcome to our newest licensed radio operator who officially joined our roster layout tracking this week:

Member Name Callsign License Class Roster Position Home Location Date Joined
Julia Elliott KR4LQQ Technician Member Surfside Beach, SC 4/21/2026

💼 Operational Notice: Club Treasurer Term Transition

A volunteer is needed to step forward for the upcoming Club Treasurer position, as Ephraim Botnick (WQ4N) is planning to step away from this role at the conclusion of his current term.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Cape Fear Cruisers Deployment, ARRL Applications & Net Rosters
item.description:
🏎️ Public Service: Cape Fear Cruisers Car Club Show

BSARC will be providing tactical radio tracking and coordinate safety communications support for the upcoming Cape Fear Cruisers Car Club Show in Southport, NC.

  • Activation Date: Saturday, April 25, 2026
  • Volunteers Needed: Please coordinate your mobile rigs and handheld check-ins with the emergency operations committee. Public events like this are highly valued opportunities to showcase amateur radio utility to county services!

🎫 Join or Renew ARRL via the Club Website

Help the club secure commissions ($15 for first-time signups, $5 for renewals) by routing your American Radio Relay League entries directly through our executive desk.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Raleigh Hamfest Recap, Bald Head Island Activation & Net Rosters
item.description:
📻 Raleigh Hamfest (RARSfest) Success

A prominent thank you to all the club members who traveled to represent BSARC at the annual RARSfest / Raleigh Hamfest this past weekend! Flea market networking and technical forums were outstanding.

🏖️ Upcoming Event: Bald Head Island Activation

Get your portable vertical antenna wires, battery boxes, and field rigs configured!

  • Activation Date: Saturday, April 11, 2026
  • Operations: The club will be setting up portable field gear layouts to activate regional grid coordinates. Monitor our digital reflector channels for exact simplex tracking and HF frequency updates as weather patterns solidify.

📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our recurring localized radio networks remain fully configured for your operational testing:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Raleigh Hamfest This Weekend, Goldsboro Unfest Preview
item.description:
🧳 This Weekend: RARSfest / The Raleigh Hamfest

Pack your travel bags and get your trade gear lists sorted out! The annual RARSfest (The Raleigh Hamfest & ARRL Roanoke Division Convention) is taking place this upcoming Saturday.

  • When: Saturday, April 4, 2026
  • Where: Raleigh, NC Fairgrounds complex mapping
  • Sponsor: Raleigh Amateur Radio Society. Check out details or grab tick logs directly at The Official RARSfest Web Desk . Safe traveling and good hunting in the flea market grids to all BSARC members heading up the highway!

📆 Preview: Wayne County Amateur Radio Club Unfest

Mark your calendar arrays for an extra regional operating track coming up next month:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Successful Experimental HF Net Run & Equipment Swapfest Market
item.description:
🎙️ Experimental HF SSB Net Log: March Session Recap

The inaugural monthly experimental HF net was successfully held at 8:00 PM on Wednesday, March 18, 2026, on 80 meters. A big thank you to Gary Quinn (NC4S) for acting as Net Control!

The following stations successfully checked in to test propagation profiles across southeastern North Carolina and beyond: K4IR, WQ4N, K1YC, KI4W, and K4JCS. We look forward to next month’s session on April 15!

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Inaugural HF SSB Net Session, April Meeting Location Switch Reminder
item.description:
📻 Reminder: Inaugural Experimental HF SSB Net Session This Week

Don’t forget to configure your radio wire dipoles and tune your HF rigs for the brand-new monthly experimental net session coordinated on behalf of Gary Quinn (NC4S):

  • When: This Wednesday night, March 18, 2026
  • Time: 8:00 PM EST
  • Frequency Target: 3.725 MHz on the 80-meter band. Come check-in and test propagation within our local area framework!

🏛️ Preview: April Monthly Meeting Switches to Calabash EMS

Please take note that our regular monthly club meeting next month will shift locations back to the beach side area. Update your navigation plans accordingly:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: March Business Meeting Tonight, New HF SSB Net Announcement
item.description:
🏛️ BSARC Monthly Club Meeting Tonight

Our monthly business assembly takes place tonight! Come out to meet the team, catch up on repeater technical configurations, and join the planning discussions for Field Day.

  • When: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 from 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
  • In-Person Venue: BEMC Headquarters — 795 Ocean Hwy W, Supply, NC 28462
  • Remote Access Notice: 🔒 Video Meeting links are distributed securely to members via the private email dispatch list to preserve server integrity.

📻 New Experimental Monthly HF SSB Net

We are excited to announce a new, experimental high-frequency operational pipeline coordinated on behalf of Gary Quinn (NC4S). This net is designed to gauge local traffic interest, test regional propagation conditions, and build community closeness:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Daylight Savings Warning, Charlotte Hamfest & Welcome New Members
item.description:
⏰ Important Notice: Daylight Savings Time Begins

Please remember to configure your station clocks and log metrics appropriately next weekend! Daylight Savings Time officially starts on Sunday, March 8, 2026. Ensure your operational schedules are adjusted for upcoming net coordination entries.

👋 Welcome Our Newest Members!

Please extend a warm Brunswick Shores welcome to our newest licensed amateur radio operators who have joined our ranks over the past few weeks:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Welcome Scott KF4SNV, March 1st NC QSO Party Kickoff Notice
item.description:
👋 Welcome Our Newest Associate Member!

Please extend a warm welcome to our newest associate who has checked into our roster layout database this week:

Member Name Call Sign License Class Roster Position Home Location Date Joined
Scott Morris KF4SNV Technician Associate Knoxville, TN & Sunset Beach, NC 02/22/2026

🎉 Next Weekend: North Carolina QSO Party Activation

Get your shacks configured, your wire tracking dipoles tuned, and your logging laptops ready to capture coordinates! The annual North Carolina QSO Party officially fires up next weekend.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: New Member Intake Logs & March Licensing Session Preview
item.description:
👋 Welcome Our Newest Licensed Member!

Please extend a warm Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club welcome to our newest licensed operator who officially joined our roster layout tracking database this week:

Member Name Call Sign License Class Roster Position Home Location Date Joined
David England N1DKE General Member Oak Island, NC & Whitefield, NH 02/17/2026

📋 Upcoming BSARC Examination & Testing Session

Ready to secure your initial FCC ticket or update your operating privileges to General or Amateur Extra? Walk-ins are fully accepted, but an early text RSVP helps our Volunteer Examiner team optimize testing computers.

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: February Monthly Meeting Notice & HamCation Kickoff
item.description:
🏛️ Preview: Upcoming February Monthly Club Meeting

Don’t forget to mark your log itineraries for our upcoming official monthly business meeting taking place this Wednesday!

  • When: Wednesday, February 11, 2026 from 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
  • In-Person Venue: BEMC Headquarters — 795 Ocean Hwy W, Supply, NC 28462
  • Remote Access Notice: 🔒 Video Meeting links are distributed securely to members via the private email dispatch list to preserve server integrity.

📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our recurring local emergency communication tracking channels remain fully configured for your local signal testing and traffic check-ins:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Winter Field Day Recap & Orlando HamCation Travel Notice
item.description:
❄️ Winter Field Day 2026 Operational Recap

A great big thank you to all club operators and technical loggers who braved the elements or checked in remotely to support our deployment at Dutchman’s Creek County Park on January 24-25! Station setup loops performed reliably, antennas handled the wind profiles cleanly, and regional simplex operations provided critical coverage mapping. Excellent public safety footprint by our emergency groups!

🧳 Regional Hamfests & conventions

  • 📆 Orlando HamCation 2026: Taking place this upcoming weekend, February 13-14, 2026 at the Central Florida Fairgrounds and Expo Park (4603 W Colonial Dr, Orlando, FL 32808)! Safe travels to all BSARC members making the trek south to catch the ARRL forums, commercial vendor arenas, and tailgate swap swap lanes. Review mapping layouts at the Official HamCation Web Desk .

📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our recurring local emergency communication tracking channels remain fully configured for your local signal testing and traffic check-ins:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Winter Field Day Thanks, Orlando HamCation Preview
item.description:
🤝 Winter Field Day: Outstanding Support and Participation

An exceptional round of appreciation and thank you to every operator, technical engineer, setup helper, and visitor who supported the club deployment at Dutchman’s Creek County Park this past weekend! Despite cold temperatures, our community turnout was brilliant. Signal tracking logs confirm that multiple voice and digital HF links were successfully maintained under challenging conditions. Well done to everyone!

🧳 Regional Hamfests & Conventions Preview

  • 📆 Orlando HamCation 2026: Mark your calendar tracking parameters for February 13-14, 2026 in Orlando, FL! Hosted at the Central Florida Fairgrounds. This stands as one of the premiere gatherings on the East Coast for vendor exhibits, ARRL technology panels, and tailgating gear swaps. Visit HamCation Website .

📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our recurring local emergency communication tracking loops remain fully configured for your local signal and traffic check-ins:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Winter Field Day This Weekend! Final Checkpoint Coordinates
item.description:
❄️ This Weekend: Winter Field Day 2026 Deployment

The wait is officially over—BSARC’s signature winter operating asset goes live this weekend! Pack your heavy winter coats, gloves, thermal layers, and portable rigs.

  • Activation Dates: This Saturday & Sunday, January 24-25, 2026
  • Footprint Coordinates: Dutchman’s Creek County Park — 4750 Fish Factory Rd SE, Southport, NC 28461
  • Schedule Loop: Heavy installation crews roll out early Saturday morning. Operations run continuously through Sunday. Talk-in frequencies will be active on the local 147.315 MHz repeater channel to assist mobile tracking units.
  • Visitors Welcome: Members, newcomers, and curious observers are strongly encouraged to come down, see our portable field stations in action, and pick up tips on off-grid battery and generator configurations!

📡 Weekly Local Radio Net Alignments

Our recurring local emergency communication tracking loops remain fully configured for your local signal and traffic check-ins:

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Monthly Business Meeting Tonight, Winter Field Day Final Logistics
item.description:
🏛️ BSARC Monthly Club Meeting Tonight

Our regular monthly business meeting takes place tonight! Come out to catch up on club financials, finalize our operational schedules, and participate in final planning for Winter Field Day deployments.

  • When: Wednesday, January 14, 2026 from 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
  • In-Person Venue: BEMC Headquarters — 795 Ocean Hwy W, Supply, NC 28462
  • Remote Access Notice: 🔒 Video Meeting links are distributed securely to members via the private email dispatch list to preserve server integrity.

🏕️ Winter Field Day Layout Planning Matrix

We are locking down the operational shifts for Dutchman’s Creek County Park. Rigs, backup power infrastructures, generator setups, and computer logging frameworks are undergoing final testing on the technical committee workbenches. Ensure you coordinate your arrival tracking with project coordinators if you plan to assist with the morning heavy antenna lifting teams!

item.title: Weekly Bulletin: Happy New Year, Winter Field Day Prep & January Meeting Preview
item.description:
🎉 Happy New Year from the Board of Directors!

Welcome to 2026! The board wishes a fantastic new year of operating, contesting, and technical experimentation to all Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club members, families, and associates. Let’s make this an outstanding year for local amateur radio infrastructure and public emergency readiness!

🏕️ Operations Check: Winter Field Day Countdown

Get your cold-weather gear sorted out, your portable power battery arrays tested, and your portable wire antennas prepped! Winter Field Day 2026 is rapidly closing in.

item.title: UUNAA Compliance
item.description:
Uniform Unincorporated Nonprofit Association Act (UUNAA)

Historical Club Compliance & Structure Documentation

Historical Note: This information tracks the legal establishment of the Brunswick Shores Amateur Radio Club (BSARC). It is provided to assist other small clubs or associations seeking a streamlined, low-cost alternative to full incorporation.

Clubs, associations, and organizations range from very small to quite large. BSARC falls squarely into the “very small” category. We operate with minimal documentation specifically tailored to our operational scope—maintaining minimal community funds, no mandatory member dues, and no complex capital assets.
